Takatsumi, Wakayama.jpg|280px]]
Mount Takatsumi where the shrine is located | location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}} Negi 1557 | coordinates = Upper shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|5.04|N|135|15|41.42|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Upper)}}
Lower shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|2.19|N|135|15|17.06|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Lower)}} | deity = {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha candidate) {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
also candidate for two small shikinaisha
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} * {{jalink|Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} * {{jalink|Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} * {{jalink|Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Takatsumi, Wakayama.jpg|280px]]
Mount Takatsumi where the shrine is located | location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}} Negi 1557 | coordinates = Upper shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|5.04|N|135|15|41.42|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Upper)}}
Lower shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|2.19|N|135|15|17.06|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Lower)}} | deity = {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha candidate) {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
also candidate for two small shikinaisha
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} * {{jalink|Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} * {{jalink|Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]]

The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. Takatsumi, Wakayama.jpg|280px]]

Lower shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|2.19|N|135|15|17.06|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Lower)}} | deity = {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}

also candidate for two small shikinaisha
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{jalink|Wakayama|Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{jalink|Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259}} * {{jalink|Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Takatsumi, Wakayama.jpg|280px]]
Mount Takatsumi where the shrine is located | location = {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747}} Negi 1557 | coordinates = Upper shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|5.04|N|135|15|41.42|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Upper)}}
Lower shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|2.19|N|135|15|17.06|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Lower)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha candidate) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Lower shrine {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|2.19|N|135|15|17.06|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Takatsumi Shrine (Lower)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}}
{{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha candidate) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
also candidate for two small shikinaisha
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha candidate) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
also candidate for two small shikinaisha
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha candidate) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
also candidate for two small shikinaisha
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]]

It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]]

Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Takasha, Takamiya, Takagozen | annual_festival = Upper shrine: April 15
Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]]

Lower shrine: October 10 }} {{座標一覧}} '''Takatsumi Shrine''' (たかつみじんじゃ; formal name 髙積神社) is a Shinto shrine in Negi, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture. It is treated as a candidate shrine either for the Meishin-taisha Tsumatsuhime Shrine or for the two small shikinaisha Takatsumihiko and Takatsumihime shrines. Its former modern rank was village shrine. The shrine has two sites on Mt. Takatsumi (237 m, also called Wasayama): the upper shrine on the summit and the lower shrine at the foot. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsutakeru-no-kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Itsutakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} ; Additional deities * Amaterasu Omikami, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Hachioji-no-kami * Oyamazumi-no-kami, Ketsuwake-no-kami, Emperor Ojin * Empress Jingu, Hime-no-Okami === History === The foundation is unknown. One major theory identifies this shrine with "Tsumatsuhime Shrine" (Meishin-taisha) in the Engishiki; another identifies it with "Takatsumihiko" and "Takatsumihime" small shrines. The first record linked to Tsumatsuhime appears in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' (702), mentioning transfer of deities including Itakiso, Ooyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime. The ''Shinsho Gecchokufu-sho'' (806) records endowments to "Tsumatobihime no Kami" in Kii. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Tsumatsuhime Shrine, Meishin-taisha, monthly and niiname offerings" is listed in Nagusa District. In a medieval water-rights dispute (1433) between Hinokuma/Kunikakasu shrines and Wasa estate, records claimed this shrine had ancient relocations and great authority. The ''Kii Zoku Fudoki'' considered identification with Tsumatsuhime highly plausible. In 1873 it was ranked village shrine. In 1910, more than ten shrines in the Wasa area, including Keshizume Shrine, were merged into Takatsumi Shrine. === Divine ranks === Records include elevations of deities identified as Tsumatsuhime, Takatsumihiko, and Takatsumihime in classical sources such as the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' and ''Kii no Kuni Jinmyocho''. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Sadashi Nakamura, "Takatsumihiko Shrine / Takatsumihime Shrine," in ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 23 (1987) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31: Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1983) === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966|1=Water dispute between Hinokuma-Kunikakasu Jingū and Taka Daimyojin}} === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takatsumi Shrine] (Wakayama Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Tsumatsuhime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University materials) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takatsumi Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名たかつみしんしや]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]]
